long_candidates.reason: 4 politicians buying MSFT: Warner, Boozman, Fetterman, Mcguire
3 politicians buying JPM: Fetterman, Keating, Khanna

decisions.reasoning: Strongest long signal in the set. 4 politicians across both chambers (Warner, Boozman, Fetterman, McGuire) buying MSFT within a ~6-week window. Warner sits on Senate Intelligence Committee with direct oversight of tech/AI policy, adding meaningful informational edge. The cluster is bipartisan and spans Senate and House, reducing the likelihood this is a purely reactionary trade to public news. Trade sizes are modest ($1K–$15K), but the breadth of actors is the real signal here. Tech sector remains in a structural uptrend, further supporting a long bias.
decisions.key_risk: Small individual trade sizes suggest these could be routine personal portfolio buys rather than conviction trades on non-public information. Cluster timing partially overlaps with broad market recovery, so this could be noise reacting to macro bounce rather than insider knowledge.

decisions.reasoning: 3 politicians buying JPM in a tight window (May 13–19), including Ro Khanna at a slightly larger size ($15K–$50K). Financials sector has benefited from deregulation tailwinds and rate environment. Khanna sits on House Armed Services and Budget committees — less direct banking oversight — and Keating is on House Foreign Affairs, so committee overlap with JPM's core business is weak. However, the cluster signal across 3 actors is real. Bipartisan mix (Fetterman, Keating, Khanna — ideologically diverse) buying the same name simultaneously is notable.
decisions.key_risk: No strong committee oversight alignment for these specific politicians. JPM is a mega-cap that many politicians routinely hold. The cluster may simply reflect a macro view on financials rather than privileged information. Lower conviction than MSFT.

decisions.reasoning: Only 2 politicians buying (Boozman and Fetterman), which is below the threshold for a confident long signal. Boozman sits on Senate Appropriations and Agriculture committees — limited direct tech oversight. Fetterman's GOOGL buy was on April 3, same date as his MSFT buy, suggesting a broad tech basket purchase rather than a targeted GOOGL thesis. The signal lacks specificity and depth to warrant opening a position.
decisions.key_risk: With only 2 actors and no strong committee oversight alignment, the risk of acting on noise is high. GOOGL also faces ongoing antitrust regulatory headwinds.

decisions.reasoning: 2 politicians buying (Boozman, Khanna) is a weak cluster. Khanna is on House Armed Services — limited direct software/creative tools oversight. Boozman's purchase falls on the same day as his GOOGL buy, again suggesting a broad tech basket rotation rather than a specific ADBE thesis. ADBE has also faced headwinds from AI disruption narratives and a failed M&A attempt. Insufficient signal strength to initiate a long.
decisions.key_risk: AI-driven disruption to ADBE's core creative software business is a structural concern. Signal is too thin to justify conviction entry.

decisions.reasoning: 2 politicians buying Constellation Energy (CEG) — Boozman and Warner — is a relatively thin cluster, but the sector context is highly compelling. CEG is a pure-play nuclear operator at the center of the AI data center power demand thesis. Warner (Senate Intelligence, Banking committees) and Boozman (Appropriations) both have visibility into federal energy and infrastructure policy, including nuclear permitting and DOE programs. The nuclear/AI power nexus is a thematic bet that aligns well with potential legislative or regulatory tailwinds these senators may be privy to. This is a BUY with moderate conviction.
decisions.key_risk: Only 2 actors, both Senate — cluster is thin. Nuclear regulatory delays or a reversal in data center power demand expectations could quickly reprice CEG. The stock has already had a significant run, increasing downside risk on a miss.

decisions.reasoning: 2 politicians selling AT&T (Fetterman on Apr 3, Gottheimer on May 20) — a weak short signal by our framework. Only 2 actors, neither with clear telecom committee oversight (Fetterman is on Senate Banking/Agriculture; Gottheimer is on House Financial Services). The sells are ~7 weeks apart, suggesting no tight coordinated cluster. T is also a high-dividend defensive stock that investors commonly trim for unrelated portfolio rebalancing reasons. Short conviction is very low — not enough to justify a short given the risks of unlimited downside.
decisions.key_risk: Shorting T carries risk of dividend yield acting as a floor for the stock price. Signal is too weak (2 actors, no committee alignment, wide time gap) to justify initiating a short position.

decisions.reasoning: 2 politicians selling AMT (Evans and McCormick, both House) within 2 days of each other — the tightest cluster among the short candidates. However, neither Evans (House Appropriations) nor McCormick (House Armed Services/Science) has meaningful oversight of telecom infrastructure REITs. AMT is also a REIT highly sensitive to interest rate expectations; selling could simply reflect a macro rate view. The signal is too weak to short — only 2 actors, no committee alignment, and REITs are commonly rebalanced in rising-rate environments for non-informational reasons.
decisions.key_risk: Shorting a REIT like AMT into a potential rate-cut cycle is dangerous — any dovish Fed pivot could cause a sharp squeeze. Short signal conviction is insufficient to overcome this macro risk.

entries_placed.reasoning: 2 politicians buying Constellation Energy (CEG) — Boozman and Warner — is a relatively thin cluster, but the sector context is highly compelling. CEG is a pure-play nuclear operator at the center of the AI data center power demand thesis. Warner (Senate Intelligence, Banking committees) and Boozman (Appropriations) both have visibility into federal energy and infrastructure policy, including nuclear permitting and DOE programs. The nuclear/AI power nexus is a thematic bet that aligns well with potential legislative or regulatory tailwinds these senators may be privy to. This is a BUY with moderate conviction.
